what_it_does: Design/UX recommendation for the 'V' symbol radial menu in a mobile interface, covering why a radial is appropriate, documented pitfalls, layout as an arc, separation of quick verbs from destination panels, icon constraints, and ambient badge signaling.
observations: Recommends a radial menu for the V symbol because equal flick distance and directional muscle memory make it faster than lists for a recurring operator.
Warns that full-circle radial menus are occluded by the holding hand/thumb and recommends a quarter/half arc anchored in the bottom corner, blooming up-and-inward into the screen.

Argues radial items should be limited to quick verbs (mode toggle, submit-all, attach, multi-select) while destinations such as supervisor chat, replies, and decisions belong in a separate panel/sheet.
Notes radial wedges must be icon-only and aligns this with Tim's preference for DNA brown-circle iconography, requiring legible 44pt glyphs.
Proposes the closed V button carry two distinct signals: a numeric draft count and a colored pip for unread replies/items needing attention.
standouts: The arc-not-full-circle layout tied specifically to one-handed thumb occlusion.
The strict split between 'do it now' radial verbs and 'go and dwell' destination panels.
The dual-badge model for the closed V: number for drafts, colored pip for needs-eyes.
conventions: Follows a structured design-argument format: benefits, documented pitfalls, constraints, and concrete recommendation.
References external UX sources by name to support claims.
Uses the ledger item id, address, and part_of link conventions.
Breaks the user's literal item list by reclassifying some entries as destinations rather than radial actions.
concerns: Terms like 'BD-G' and 'MSG2' are cited without being defined in this file.
The examples 'attach' and 'multi-select' are introduced as quick verbs though not in Tim's original stated list.
No concrete icon glyphs or visual specs are provided beyond the 44pt target.
The draft-count and unread-replies data sources and update semantics are not specified.
notes: A UX rationale that wants to keep the V radial menu but reshapes it into an arc of quick verbs plus a destination sheet, with a two-signal badge on the closed button.
questions: What are 'BD-G' and 'MSG2' and where are they documented?
What is the exact set of quick-verb icons in the DNA brown-circle iconography?
How are pending-draft count and unread-replies computed and refreshed?
What is the parent item-a5b7cf79 and how does this note fit into the broader P5 thread?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title frames a radial-menu recommendation with pitfalls, and the body delivers exactly that analysis plus concrete layout and signaling guidance.
apparent_themes: Gesture learnability and expert marking menus
Ambient status signaling on a single persistent control
intention_signals: It is trying to defend the radial-menu direction for the V symbol while steering the design away from common radial-menu failures by limiting scope, shaping the arc, and offloading destinations to a sheet.
novelty: The specific proposal of a bottom-anchored arc blooming up-and-inward to avoid one-handed occlusion, and the closed-V dual-badge semantics distinguishing drafts from needs-eyes.
contribution: A reusable set of radial-menu UX constraints: the arc layout rule, the verb/destination split, the icon-only thumb-size requirement, and the dual-badge status model for a persistent action button.
summary_for_embedding: A design note recommending a radial menu for the 'V' symbol, arguing for a bottom-anchored arc that avoids thumb occlusion, separating quick verbs from destination panels, using DNA brown-circle iconography, and showing draft count plus a colored needs-eyes pip on the closed button.
intention_for_embedding: To guide implementation of the V radial menu by balancing gesture learnability against documented radial-menu pitfalls and Tim's icon/signal constraints.
